name: Generation-limit event ends — solar must recover
hypothesis: A generation-limit event curtails the inverter, then expires. The hub must actively release the ceiling — the known stuck-curtailment failure leaves an inverter clamped at its last limit after the event clears.
expected: When the cap lifts, solar climbs back to its full potential within seconds. It must never be left clamped below potential after the event ends.
fix: Restore must SET the ceiling to full nameplate, not emit an empty no-op (solarCommandToControl restore path). NOTE: a true device dropout (Modbus link loss) needs the sim's service stopped via SSH — out of scope for this in-process injector.
notes: TASK-077: migrated from the Go literal in cmd/dashboard/mayhem.go scenarios() (ID curtailment-release). suppress_default clears the program-0 default (5 kW export cap) so 'release' means 'unconstrained' — otherwise the hub's correct fallback to the default holds solar under the diagnoseRecovery >=95% bar and fakes a stuck ceiling (see mayhem_world.go's suppressDefault doc comment; QA v5 artifact). HoldS 60: long tail so a slow ramp can be told apart from a true stuck restore. The constraint's own hold_s (15) is the cap's short duration inside the 60 s observation window, released early at tick 15.
